key_issues: S5 never assigns ownership of the `initialize` handler / server-side negotiation to the low-level Server (TS server.ts owns it); as written the S5 client connect has nothing to handshake against and the demo would not run.

S6 is too big: bundles all real transports (M7 stdio + M8 Streamable HTTP with N2 resumption + M9 web-server adapter) AND the full high-level server (M12b+M12c, pagination, handle lifecycle). Split into S6a (transports) and S6b (high-level server); both already drawn as separate graph nodes.

S7 is too big: sampling + elicitation + roots + per-session subscription fan-out (J1) + client pagination (J2) + per-session logging filter (S3) + progress/cancel wiring is 6-7 distinct flows. Split into S7a (client-driven server-initiated flows + cursor-following) and S7b (server session-state: subscriptions, logging filter, progress/cancel).

S5 cross-SDK interop gate is non-binary ('over in-memory/stdio bridging where feasible ... else document the bridge used and defer'); the project's first-interop milestone can pass without achieving interop. Make it binary: pull a minimal stdio client into S5 or move the first cross-SDK leg to S6a.

S1 omits core/types/errors.ts (the wire-error DECODE module that turns a -32042 payload into UrlElicitationRequiredError); architecture 4.1 and S1's own test criterion require both-direction exn<->JSON-RPC mapping. Add it to the M2 source list and test the decode path.
No stage assigns the server side that RAISES -32042 UrlElicitationRequired (mcp.ts:156); only the client receive side is described in S7.

Under-specified acceptance gates: S2 default JSON-Schema validator subset has no minimum keyword bar; S9 'five example categories' undersells the ~17 server + ~13 client TS examples and omits the inMemoryEventStore resumption example that exercises N2's pluggable-store seam.
Minor sequencing: M5e stdio framing is built in S2 but explicitly unused until S6 (orphaned deliverable); transport-port `related-request-id` on send is never exercised before HTTP.
